name: code-terminal-run
title: Code Terminal Run
description: A token-chrome terminal panel runs one command: the prompt line types deterministically behind an integer-cycle caret, executes after a beat, and output lines print one per cue before a fresh prompt appears.
evidence: Wave J2 J2-7 code-terminal-run specification; the typed-prompt text-at-time law generalized to rich authored spans (character reveal across text nodes preserves token coloring)

variables.label: Prompt glyph
variables.description: Leading glyph before the command and the trailing prompt. Empty string hides it.

variables.label: Cadence
variables.description: Keystroke rhythm: uniform fixed steps or seeded human chunks.

variables.label: Cues
variables.description: Comma-separated seconds relative to mount start; cue N sets when output line N prints. Empty uses the authored rhythm.

variables.label: Accent
variables.description: Color of the prompt glyph and caret.

variables.label: Exit
variables.description: Outgoing transition. Frame roots own transitions; none holds the end frame.
